meta.title: Streptosporangium subroseum sp. nov., an actinomycete with an unusual phospholipid pattern
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: A strain of Streptosporangium with an unusual phospholipid pattern was isolated from Yunnan Province, a region in China. The isolate, designated CY-7113(T), was identified by morphological and physiological properties, cell chemistry, genomic DNA G+C content, DNA--DNA hybridization and phylogenetic analysis. Based on 16S rRNA gene sequencing, DNA--DNA hybridization, phenotypic characteristics and its unusual phospholipid pattern, it was concluded that strain CY-7113(T) belongs to a novel Streptosporangium species, for which the name Streptosporangium subroseum sp. nov. is proposed. Strain CY-7113(T) (=CCTCC 97008(T)=CRC 16302(T)) is the type strain.
